revision.comment: 新しいページ：湊茶臼山古墳（岡山県岡山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 湊茶臼山古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 52.04 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 57|経度秒 = 15.11 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m
高さ11m（後円部） |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 中心主体：（推定）なし
副次第1主体：木棺直葬
副次第2主体：[[粘土槨]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[埴輪]]・土器 |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 湊茶臼山
古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''湊茶臼山古墳'''（みなとちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地から東方の、操山丘陵南部の山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。一帯では、金蔵山古墳（墳丘長165メートル）・網浜茶臼山古墳（墳丘長92メートル）などの古墳200基以上からなる[[操山古墳群]]の分布が知られる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。古墳域では[[2008年|2008]]-[[2011年]]度（[[平成]]20-23年度）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方に向ける。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。また前方部北側では方形壇（島状遺構）、前方部前端側では周堤状遺構が認められている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。主体部の埋葬施設としては、中心的主体は欠くとみられ、副次的主体は木棺直葬（第1主体）・[[粘土槨]]（第2主体）の2基が認められる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。出土品としては、[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪や、[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・草摺形埴輪）、[[土師器]]、陶質土器（推定）、鉄鏃（第1主体より）などが検出されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 この湊茶臼山古墳は、出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。古墳の南側には海が広がることから、築造の背景には海上交通との関わりが推測される{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。しかし大型古墳ながら中心埋葬が存在しないと見られ、全体としても未完成の様相が強いことから、背景となる当時の社会情勢・古墳祭祀を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 * 墳丘長：約125メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約72メートル ** 高さ：約11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：約60メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル 墳丘周囲にはテラス状の段が巡らされており、この段を含む全長は約150メートルに及ぶ{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設に関して、本古墳は中心的主体を欠くものと推測されている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。これは、後円部墳頂の中央部において巨大な盗掘坑（直径約3.5メートル・深さ3.5メートル）が認められたものの施設の痕跡が全く検出されず、中央以外に設けられた埋葬施設2基（後述）も古墳規模には見合わないことによる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。墳丘に葺石が無い点や、埴輪が少ない点においても、本古墳が未完成な様が指摘される{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 上のように中心的主体を欠く一方、後円部墳頂では副次的な埋葬施設2基が認められている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。これらはいずれも墳丘主軸と平行するが、後円部中心からずれて位置する{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。第1主体は[[割竹形木棺]]（長さ4.5メートル・幅2メートル）の直葬で、第2主体は割竹形木棺が粘土で覆われた[[粘土槨]]（長さ7メートル・幅2メートル）になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000059842.pdf 第1次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査現地説明会資料|2008年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference= 第2次調査現地説明会資料|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 - [http://www.city.okayama.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_00005.html リンク先参照]（岡山市ホームページ）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000080817.pdf 第3次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000106472.pdf 第4次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2011年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター編 |author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=湊茶臼山古墳 範囲確認調査報告書|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:みなとちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

副次第2主体：[[粘土槨]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[埴輪]]・土器 |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 湊茶臼山
古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''湊茶臼山古墳'''（みなとちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地から東方の、[[旭川 (岡山県)|旭川]]東岸の操山丘陵南部の山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。一帯では、金蔵山古墳（墳丘長165メートル）・網浜茶臼山古墳（墳丘長92メートル）などの古墳200基以上からなる[[操山古墳群]]の分布が知られる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。古墳域では[[2008年|2008]]-[[2011年]]度（[[平成]]20-23年度）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方に向ける。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。また前方部北側では方形壇（島状遺構）、前方部前端側では周堤状遺構が認められている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。主体部の埋葬施設としては、中心的主体は欠くとみられ、副次的主体は木棺直葬（第1主体）・[[粘土槨]]（第2主体）の2基が認められる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。出土品としては、[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪や、[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・草摺形埴輪）、[[土師器]]、陶質土器（推定）、鉄鏃（第1主体より）などが検出されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 この湊茶臼山古墳は、出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。古墳の南側には海が広がることから、築造の背景には海上交通との関わりが推測される{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。しかし大型古墳ながら中心埋葬が存在しないと見られ、全体としても未完成の様相が強いことから、背景となる当時の社会情勢・古墳祭祀を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 * 墳丘長：約125メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約72メートル ** 高さ：約11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：約60メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル 墳丘周囲にはテラス状の段が巡らされており、この段を含む全長は約150メートルに及ぶ{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設に関して、本古墳は中心的主体を欠くものと推測されている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。これは、後円部墳頂の中央部において巨大な盗掘坑（直径約3.5メートル・深さ3.5メートル）が認められたものの施設の痕跡が全く検出されず、中央以外に設けられた埋葬施設2基（後述）も古墳規模には見合わないことによる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。墳丘に葺石が無い点や、埴輪が少ない点においても、本古墳が未完成な様が指摘される{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 上のように中心的主体を欠く一方、後円部墳頂では副次的な埋葬施設2基が認められている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。これらはいずれも墳丘主軸と平行するが、後円部中心からずれて位置する{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。第1主体は[[割竹形木棺]]（長さ4.5メートル・幅2メートル）の直葬で、第2主体は割竹形木棺が粘土で覆われた[[粘土槨]]（長さ7メートル・幅2メートル）になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000059842.pdf 第1次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査現地説明会資料|2008年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference= 第2次調査現地説明会資料|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 - [http://www.city.okayama.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_00005.html リンク先参照]（岡山市ホームページ）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000080817.pdf 第3次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000106472.pdf 第4次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011年}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2011年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター編 |author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=湊茶臼山古墳 範囲確認調査報告書|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 湊茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 52.04 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 57|経度秒 = 15.11 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m
高さ11m（後円部） |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 中心主体：（推定）なし
副次第1主体：木棺直葬
副次第2主体：[[粘土槨]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[埴輪]]・土器 |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 湊茶臼山
古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''湊茶臼山古墳'''（みなとちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地から東方の、[[旭川 (岡山県)|旭川]]東岸の[[操山]]丘陵南部の山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。操山では本古墳のほかにも、[[金蔵山古墳]]（165メートル）・[[網浜茶臼山古墳]]（92メートル）などの古墳200基以上の分布が知られる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。古墳域では[[2008年|2008]]-[[2011年]]度（[[平成]]20-23年度）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方に向ける。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。また前方部北側では方形壇（島状遺構）、前方部前端側では周堤状遺構が認められている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。主体部の埋葬施設としては、中心的主体は欠くとみられ、副次的主体は木棺直葬（第1主体）・[[粘土槨]]（第2主体）の2基が認められる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。出土品としては、[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪や、[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・草摺形埴輪）、[[土師器]]、陶質土器（推定）、鉄鏃（第1主体より）などが検出されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 この湊茶臼山古墳は、出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。古墳の南側には海が広がることから、築造の背景には海上交通との関わりが推測される{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。しかし大型古墳ながら中心埋葬が存在しないと見られ、全体としても未完成の様相が強いことから、背景となる当時の社会情勢・古墳祭祀を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 * 墳丘長：約125メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約72メートル ** 高さ：約11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：約60メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル 墳丘周囲にはテラス状の段が巡らされており、この段を含む全長は約150メートルに及ぶ{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設に関して、本古墳は中心的主体を欠くものと推測されている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これは、後円部墳頂の中央部において巨大な盗掘坑（直径約3.5メートル・深さ3.5メートル）が認められたものの施設の痕跡が全く検出されず、中央以外に設けられた埋葬施設2基（後述）も古墳規模には見合わないことによる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。墳丘に葺石が無い点や、埴輪が少ない点においても、本古墳が未完成な様が指摘される{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 上のように中心的主体を欠く一方、後円部墳頂では副次的な埋葬施設2基が認められている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これらはいずれも墳丘主軸と平行するが、後円部中心からずれて位置する{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。第1主体は[[割竹形木棺]]（長さ4.5メートル・幅2メートル）の直葬で、第2主体は割竹形木棺が粘土で覆われた[[粘土槨]]（長さ7メートル・幅2メートル）になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000059842.pdf 第1次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査現地説明会資料|2008}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference= 第2次調査現地説明会資料|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 - [http://www.city.okayama.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_00005.html リンク先参照]（岡山市ホームページ）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000080817.pdf 第3次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000106472.pdf 第4次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2011年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター編 |author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=湊茶臼山古墳 範囲確認調査報告書|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:みなとちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''湊茶臼山古墳'''（みなとちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地から東方の、[[旭川 (岡山県)|旭川]]東岸の[[操山]]丘陵南部の山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。操山では本古墳のほかにも、[[金蔵山古墳]]（165メートル）・[[網浜茶臼山古墳]]（92メートル）などの古墳200基以上の分布が知られる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。古墳域では[[2008年|2008]]-[[2011年]]度（[[平成]]20-23年度）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方に向ける。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。また前方部北側では方形壇（島状遺構）、前方部前端側では周堤状遺構が認められている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。主体部の埋葬施設としては、中心的主体は欠くとみられ、副次的主体は木棺直葬（第1主体）・[[粘土槨]]（第2主体）の2基が認められる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。出土品としては、[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪や、[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・草摺形埴輪）、[[土師器]]、陶質土器（推定）、鉄鏃（第1主体より）などが検出されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 この湊茶臼山古墳は、出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。古墳の南側には海が広がることから、築造の背景には海上交通との関わりが推測される{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。しかし大型古墳ながら中心埋葬が存在しないと見られ、全体としても未完成の様相が強いことから、背景となる当時の社会情勢・古墳祭祀を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 * 墳丘長：約125メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約72メートル ** 高さ：約11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：約60メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル 墳丘周囲にはテラス状の段が巡らされており、この段を含む全長は約150メートルに及ぶ{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設に関して、本古墳は中心的主体を欠くものと推測されている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これは、後円部墳頂の中央部において巨大な盗掘坑（直径約3.5メートル・深さ3.5メートル）が認められたものの施設の痕跡が全く検出されず、中央以外に設けられた埋葬施設2基（後述）も古墳規模には見合わないことによる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。墳丘に葺石が無い点や、埴輪が少ない点においても、本古墳が未完成な様が指摘される{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 上のように中心的主体を欠く一方、後円部墳頂では副次的な埋葬施設2基が認められている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これらはいずれも墳丘主軸と平行するが、後円部中心からずれて位置する{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。第1主体は[[割竹形木棺]]（長さ4.5メートル・幅2メートル）の直葬で、第2主体は割竹形木棺が粘土で覆われた[[粘土槨]]（長さ7メートル・幅2メートル）になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000059842.pdf 第1次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査現地説明会資料|2008}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference= 第2次調査現地説明会資料|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 - [http://www.city.okayama.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_00005.html リンク先参照]（岡山市ホームページ）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000080817.pdf 第3次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000106472.pdf 第4次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2011年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=湊茶臼山古墳 範囲確認調査報告書|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:みなとちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''湊茶臼山古墳'''（みなとちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]湊にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地から東方の、[[旭川 (岡山県)|旭川]]東岸の[[操山]]丘陵南部の山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。操山では本古墳のほかにも、[[金蔵山古墳]]（165メートル）・[[網浜茶臼山古墳]]（92メートル）などの古墳200基以上の分布が知られる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。[[2008年|2008]]-[[2011年]]度（[[平成]]20-23年度）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。墳丘外表で[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。また前方部北側では方形壇（島状遺構）、前方部前端側では周堤状遺構が認められている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。埋葬施設としては、中心的主体は欠くとみられ、副次的主体は木棺直葬（第1主体）・[[粘土槨]]（第2主体）の2基が認められる{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。出土品としては、[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪や、[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・草摺形埴輪）、[[土師器]]、陶質土器（推定）、鉄鏃（第1主体より）などが検出されている{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。古墳の南側には海が広がることから、築造の背景には海上交通との関わりが推測される{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。しかし大型古墳ながら中心埋葬が存在しないと見られ、全体としても未完成の様相が強いことから、背景となる当時の社会情勢・古墳祭祀を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 * 墳丘長：約125メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約72メートル ** 高さ：約11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：約60メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル 墳丘周囲にはテラス状の段が巡らされており、この段を含む全長は約150メートルに及ぶ{{Sfn|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}。 Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|後円部墳頂 Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|[[岡山シティミュージアム]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設に関して、本古墳は中心的主体を欠くものと推測されている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これは、後円部墳頂の中央部において巨大な盗掘坑（直径約3.5メートル・深さ3.5メートル）が認められたものの施設の痕跡が全く検出されず、中央以外に設けられた埋葬施設2基（後述）も古墳規模には見合わないことによる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。墳丘に葺石が無い点や、埴輪が少ない点においても、本古墳が未完成な様が指摘される{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 中心的主体を欠く一方、後円部墳頂では副次的な埋葬施設2基が認められている{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。これらはいずれも墳丘主軸と平行するが、後円部中心からずれて位置する{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。第1主体は[[割竹形木棺]]（長さ4.5メートル・幅2メートル）の直葬で、第2主体は割竹形木棺が粘土で覆われた[[粘土槨]]（長さ7メートル・幅2メートル）になる{{Sfn|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}。 湊茶臼山古墳 鉄鏃.JPG|鉄鏃
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000059842.pdf 第1次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査現地説明会資料|2008}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference= 第2次調査現地説明会資料|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 - [http://www.city.okayama.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_00005.html リンク先参照]（岡山市ホームページ）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000080817.pdf 第3次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000106472.pdf 第4次調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査現地説明会資料|2011}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2011年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=湊茶臼山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]34|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 湊茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|湊茶臼山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=湊茶臼山古墳 範囲確認調査報告書|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html 湊茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:みなとちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Tombs |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: * Diameter: ~72 meters * Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: * Width: ~60 meters * Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * **Secondary Burial 1**: A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * **Secondary Burial 2**: A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * **Secondary Burial 1**: A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * **Secondary Burial 2**: A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: * Diameter: ~72 meters * Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: * Width: ~60 meters * Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tombs]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Naka-ku, Okayama]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tombs]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tombs]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Naka-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tombs]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]]

Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tombs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Naka-ku, Okayama]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tombs]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]]

The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
