{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.11 |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City {{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~qq meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~qq meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City {{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~qq meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~qq meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~qq meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 burial mounds, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds|ja|続 Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|続 View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|中区}} Minato |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 38 |latitude_sec = 52.04 |longitude_deg = 133 |longitude_min = 57 |longitude_sec = 15.qq |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: qqm (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} - early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} |buried = Unknown |artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |icon=Keyhole-shaped tomb |label_position = top }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区}} in {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Sozan Hills|ja|操山}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 20qq, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb Structure === The tomb features a keyhole shape, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular platform (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === Burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary Burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary Burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
