{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28690507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|qq=|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|qq=|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|qq=|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|湊茶臼山古墳|version=101786613|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28690507}}

View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

[[sn:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28690507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|湊茶臼山古墳|version=101786613|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28690507}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q28690507}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

[[commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28690507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

[[commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28690507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]] [[:en:Central District|Naka-ku, Okayama]] Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The tomb is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|湊茶臼山古墳|version=101786613|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28690507}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Minato Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28690507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Minato Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (foreground: rectangular section; background: circular section) |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City}} {{ill|Central District|en|Central District|WD=Q3710518|lt=Naka-ku, Okayama}} Minato |coordinates = {{Coord|34.647789|N|133.954197|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |iso = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Total length: 125m
Height: 11m (circular mound) |era = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} - early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Central structure: None (presumed)
Secondary burial 1: Direct burial in a wooden coffin
Secondary burial 2: {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |buried = Unknown |burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, pottery |historic_sites = None |notes = |map=Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Minato Chausuyama
Kofun |type=Keyhole-shaped tomb |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Minato Chausuyama Kofun''' (湊茶臼山古墳) is an ancient tomb located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|ja|岡山市中区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-ku, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中区 (岡山市)|1=Naka-ku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q852116}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. The tomb is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tomb was built at the summit of a hill in the southern portion of the {{ill|Mount Misao|lt=Mount Misao|lt=Sozan Hills|ja|操山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sozan Hills|qq=}}, located on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Asahi River (Okayama)|ja|旭川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi River (Okayama)|1=Asahigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306312}} in southern Okayama Prefecture, east of the urban center of Okayama City. Over 200 Kofuns, including the {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kanakurayama Kofun|lt=Kinzanzan Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinzanzan Kofun|qq=}} (165 meters) and the {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (92 meters), are distributed across the Sozan Hills. The Minato Chausuyama Kofun underwent excavations between 2008 and 2011, uncovering details about its structure and burial practices. === Tomb burial === The tomb features a keyhole type, with the rectangular section oriented northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers in the circular section and two tiers in the rectangular section. Uniquely, there are no indications of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} on the mound’s surface. A rectangular plattype (island-shaped structure) was identified on the northern side of the rectangular section, and a ridge-like structure was confirmed at its front edge. The mound measures: * Total length: ~125 meters * Circular section: ** Diameter: ~72 meters ** Height: ~11 meters * Rectangular section: ** Width: ~60 meters ** Height: ~5 meters Terraces surround the mound, extending its overall length to approximately 150 meters. === burial Features === The central burial chamber is presumed to be absent. This conclusion is based on the presence of a large looting pit (3.5 meters in diameter and depth) at the center of the circular mound, which revealed no traces of burial structures. Two secondary burial facilities were identified, both aligned parallel to the mound’s axis but slightly offset from the center. * '''Secondary burial 1''': A direct burial using a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (~4.5 meters long, ~2 meters wide). * '''Secondary burial 2''': A {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, approximately 7 meters long and 2 meters wide, enclosing a similar coffin. burial goods recovered include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, grass-skirt-shaped haniwa, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, and iron arrowheads. == Historical Significance == The Minato Chausuyama Kofun dates to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the transition from the early to middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Its construction near the coast suggests a connection to maritime activities. However, its incomplete state and absence of a central burial chamber raise questions about the sociopolitical dynamics and ritual practices of the time. == Gallery == Minato Chausuyama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Circular mound apex Minato Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the rectangular section to the circular section Minato Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the circular section to the rectangular section 湊茶臼山古墳 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja|岡山県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Historical and Cultural Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1988|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kusahara Takanori|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/04.html Minato Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|湊茶臼山古墳|version=101786613|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湊茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Minato Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28690507}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
